underlying: Roku Inc. total platform Streaming Hours in Q1 2026 (billions)
resolution_time: 2026-04-30T21:00:00-04:00 (Roku Q1 2026 earnings call scheduled 2pm PT = 5pm ET on Apr 30, 2026)
method: Normal CDF on Q1 2026 hours streamed, mu=40.0B sigma=1.0B. Anchored on Roku's reported quarterly hours: Q1 2025 = 35.8B (+5.1B YoY = +16.7%), Q2 2025 = 35.4B (+17.2% YoY), Q3 2025 = 36.5B (+14.1% YoY), Q4 2025 = 37.9B (FY2025 145.6B implied, +13.0% YoY). Recent QoQ trajectory shows decelerating but still solid double-digit YoY hours growth; trend slope ~13-14% YoY entering Q1 2026. Apply ~12% YoY = 40.1B central. Q1 2026 revenue guidance is +18% YoY ($1.2B) which is somewhat above hours trend; engagement growth typically lags revenue (CTV ad rate increases). Sigma 1.0B reflects: (a) one-quarter-out uncertainty in YoY hours growth (recent quarters show ~3pp swing in YoY%, =~1B hours), (b) seasonality drift (Q1 typically not peak), (c) no public preview of the metric before the print. Not yet disclosed — earnings on Apr 30 2026 ~5pm ET. Market mid-quotes (T38.5 0.855, T39 0.74, T39.5 0.415, T40 0.185, T40.5 0.09) imply a market mu near 39.5-39.7B with sigma ~0.9B; my mu is 0.3-0.5B above market, which biases YES on lower strikes vs. mid quotes.
notes: Q1 2026 Roku hours streamed market — earnings drop Apr 30 2026 (2 days post as_of). Hours metric is disclosed every quarter in the shareholder letter. Historical anchors verified from PDFs (Q1/Q2/Q3 2025 letters and Q4 2025 letter on roku.com). Q1 2026 +12% YoY on 35.8B base = 40.1B central; sigma 1.0B chosen so T38.0 yes ~0.98 and T40.5 yes ~0.31 — a balanced normal around the most-likely outcome with non-tiny tails on either side. Confidence medium-low because (i) no Q1 2026 public preview, (ii) macro/cord-cutting/ad-spend variance can shift hours +/- 1-2B from trend, (iii) Q1 seasonality less reliable than streaming-heavy Q4. If pre-earnings leak or analyst preview surfaces a different number, theo should be re-anchored.
